name: Therme Erding - Sauna Paradise
highlights: The world's largest sauna paradise with 35 saunas and steam baths near Munich, including the world's largest event sauna seating 200 people. The textile-free VitalOase features 20 lovingly themed saunas with elaborate Aufguss ceremonies. A massive complex that draws sauna enthusiasts from across Europe.

name: Friedrichsbad
hours: Daily 08:00-22:00, last admission 3 hours before closing
highlights: A stunning Renaissance-style bathhouse opened in 1877, once considered the most modern in Europe. Features a unique 17-stage Roman-Irish bathing ritual combining warm air baths, steam rooms, thermal pools, and cold plunges. Mark Twain famously said after visiting: 'After 10 minutes you forget time, after 20 minutes the world.'

name: Müller'sches Volksbad
highlights: Munich's first public indoor swimming pool, opened in 1901 in magnificent Art Nouveau style designed by Carl Hocheder. Features a Roman-Irish steam bath that slowly warms the body through rooms at 40°C, 60°C, and 80°C, plus a Finnish sauna with hourly Aufguss. The baroque staircase, wall murals, stucco and bronze statues make bathing here a truly artistic experience.

name: Liquidrom
highlights: A futuristic urban spa in central Berlin near Potsdamer Platz, famous for its saltwater floating pool under a striking concrete dome with underwater music and light shows. Features Finnish, Himalayan salt, and Kelo herbal saunas. DJs provide ambient music on select evenings, creating a uniquely Berlin intersection of wellness and nightlife culture.

name: Neptunbad
highlights: A beautifully restored 1912 Art Nouveau bathhouse in Cologne's Ehrenfeld district, combining historic architecture with modern wellness. Features multiple saunas, steam baths, indoor and outdoor pools, a rooftop terrace, and a serene garden. The blend of century-old tile work and contemporary spa design makes it one of Cologne's most atmospheric sauna experiences.

name: Claudius Therme
highlights: Nestled in the greenery of Cologne's Rheinpark with views of the famous cathedral across the Rhine. Features three large sauna areas: indoor saunas, a traditional outdoor sauna village with log cabins, and the Rose Terraces. Includes Finnish saunas, Russian banya, and a wide variety of steam baths with regular Aufguss ceremonies.

name: Mediterana
highlights: An expansive 52,000 m² wellness retreat near Cologne featuring 13 uniquely themed sauna and bath creations inspired by cultures worldwide, from Roman baths to Oriental hammam. The beautifully landscaped gardens and themed relaxation areas create distinct atmospheric zones. Known for exceptional Aufguss ceremonies and elaborate design throughout.

name: Kaifu-Bad
highlights: Hamburg's oldest bath features a 600 m² sauna area across two levels with a classic Finnish sauna, dry sauna, color light sauna, and Roman-Irish steam bath. A beloved neighborhood institution in the Eimsbüttel district. Tuesdays are women-only sauna days, reflecting the inclusive community spirit of Hamburg's public bathing culture.

name: Holthusenbad
highlights: A comprehensive thermal bath and sauna complex in Hamburg's upscale Eppendorf district with nine different sauna facilities ranging from 45°C to 100°C. Includes steam baths, relaxation rooms, and a swimming pool. The variety of temperatures and sauna types makes it one of Hamburg's most popular public sauna destinations, appealing to both beginners and experienced sauna-goers.

name: Taunus Therme
address: Seedammweg 10, 61352 Bad Homburg vor der Höhe
highlights: A sprawling spa resort near Frankfurt featuring over 1,400 m² of water surfaces including indoor/outdoor pools and a swimming lake, plus 8 themed saunas in the mixed area and 2 in the women-only zone. The oriental '1001 Nights' dream world and hamam area transport visitors to another era. Set in a natural landscape at the foot of the Taunus mountains.

name: SchwabenQuellen
highlights: A 7,000 m² sauna and wellness paradise in Stuttgart's SI-Centrum featuring 18 distinct themed areas inspired by cultures from around the world, from Japanese onsen to Native American sweat lodges. Known for elaborate and creative Aufguss ceremonies performed by trained sauna masters. The diversity of themed environments makes each visit a cultural journey.

name: Europabad Karlsruhe
hours: Varies; sauna closes 30 min before pool closing
highlights: Renowned for its exceptional Aufguss ceremonies across five saunas, highlighted by the Kalevala sauna in a Karelian-Finnish sauna village where Wenik (birch branch) rituals are performed. The facility bridges Finnish and German sauna cultures authentically, with wood-fired elements and a strong focus on traditional steam ceremonies. Consistently ranked among Baden-Württemberg's best sauna experiences.

name: Thermen & Badewelt Sinsheim
highlights: Home to 13 spectacular themed sauna attractions including what was once the world's largest sauna, set alongside the Palmenparadies (Palm Paradise) with over 400 genuine palm trees and turquoise lagoons. The diverse Aufguss program across seven saunas is among the most varied in Germany. Stunning tropical architecture creates a year-round summer escape.

name: Sachsen Therme
highlights: Leipzig's premier thermal and sauna complex with a 3,000 m² sauna paradise featuring eight artistically themed saunas decorated with precious wall paintings. The facility combines swimming, wellness and sauna culture under one roof. A popular destination for both locals and visitors to Saxony seeking a full day of relaxation.

name: Georg-Arnhold-Bad Sauna
highlights: Dresden's most popular public sauna, branded as 'Sweating between Alte Meister' (Old Masters) in a nod to the nearby Gemäldegalerie. Features themed sauna rooms in a modern facility with both indoor and outdoor options. The entrance fee includes access to the outdoor swimming pool, making it a great value for a full day out in the Saxon capital.

name: Vabali Spa Düsseldorf
highlights: A 20,000 m² Balinese-inspired wellness oasis on the shores of Elbsee lake, featuring 14 saunas and steam baths, multiple pools and soaking tubs, and an expansive sauna garden with panoramic lake views. The Asian-Mediterranean architecture, two fireplace rooms, and serene garden areas create an escape that feels worlds away from the city. Widely regarded as one of Germany's most beautiful sauna landscapes.

name: SATAMA Sauna Resort & Spa
hours: Daily, check website for seasonal hours
highlights: A lakeside sauna park set among ancient trees on the sunny southern shore of Lake Scharmützelsee in Brandenburg. Features 9 different saunas and steam baths with direct lake access for cold plunges. Host of the German Aufguss Championships and Aufguss World Championships — considered one of the most prestigious sauna venues in the country for serious sauna culture.

name: Obermain Therme
highlights: A sprawling Franconian thermal complex with over 25 pools both indoor and outdoor, a natural bathing lake, and eleven diverse saunas arranged around the lake in the Bavarian countryside. The combination of authentic thermal water from deep underground springs with lakeside sauna cabins creates one of Germany's most scenic and natural sauna environments. A genuine hidden gem away from tourist crowds.

name: Caracalla Spa
highlights: A modern thermal bath fed by Baden-Baden's famous natural hot springs, complementing the historic Friedrichsbad next door. Multiple indoor and outdoor thermal pools at varying temperatures are surrounded by a generous sauna area. Tuesday evenings offer a special late deal — bathe and sauna from 7 PM and only pay for 2 hours. The perfect companion visit to Friedrichsbad for a full Baden-Baden thermal experience.

name: Therme Bad Wörishofen
highlights: An Allgäu wellness gem featuring 15 uniquely themed saunas including the Löyly-Hütte heated above 90°C, Roman-style saunas, and rustic Alpine wood-fired cabins. Set in the Bavarian countryside near the Alps, the facility draws on the Kneipp health tradition of the town's famous priest Sebastian Kneipp. The variety spans from mild herbal saunas to intense Finnish-style heat.

name: Kristall Palm Beach
highlights: Near Nuremberg, this extensive water park and sauna complex features an outdoor sauna village designed as a small Alpine hamlet with log cabin saunas, outdoor Jacuzzis, and Egyptian/Oriental themed indoor areas. The contrast between the rustic Bavarian outdoor village and the exotic indoor themed saunas offers surprising variety. A solid option for the Nuremberg/Franconia region.

name: Niederrhein-Therme
highlights: A no-nonsense public thermal and sauna complex with 15 different saunas, 32°C mineral brine baths, adventure showers, crushed ice fountains, and a large sauna garden with plunge pool. Known for hosting special themed Saturday sauna nights that attract hardcore sauna enthusiasts. Excellent value for money and a strong community-oriented atmosphere — a favorite among serious Ruhr-area sauna-goers.

name: Limes-Therme
highlights: The only bath in Bavaria with all three natural health resources: mineral thermal water, natural moor, and sulfur springs. The Römer-Sauna world is housed in a reconstructed Roman Limes Tower and bathhouse, directly referencing the ancient Roman frontier that once ran through here. An authentically historical sauna experience rooted in 2,000 years of bathing tradition on the Danube.

name: Vabali Spa Hamburg
highlights: The newest addition to the Vabali chain, featuring the signature Balinese-inspired design with multiple saunas and steam baths, nap lounges, pools, soaking tubs, and extensive garden areas. All admission includes full facility access. Reservations are strongly encouraged especially on weekends as the venue frequently reaches capacity — a testament to its popularity in the Hamburg region.
